figures.caption: Fig. 2. Qualitative results. (a) MouseEmbryo after rigid ICP pre-alignment; (b) INSTAlign non-rigid alignment on the same pair; (c) 3D reconstruction of DLPFC Sample 3 from consecutively aligned slices, colored by cortical layer.

Table 1. D1–D3: DLPFC samples; MB2/MB7/MB11: MERFISH Brain sections; ST: STARMap; MH: MERFISH Hypothalamus; ME: MouseEmbryo. Spatial alignment results across nine datasets. Best in bold, second-best underlined. “–” indicates method not available or no alignment.

Fig. 1. Overview of the INST-Align pipeline. Phase 1 learns a shared canonical expression field fθ as an implicit neural representation that maps spatial coordinates to embedding vectors and reconstructs gene expression profiles. Phase 2 jointly optimizes a non-rigid deformation network and feature-guided spatial matching within the shared field, enabling mutually reinforcing alignment and representation learning under smoothness regularization.
